Recode raw physician credential strings to MD, DO, or Other

Source: R/factor_utils.R
mysterycall_recode_credentials.Rd

Normalises the messy free-text values found in NPPES Provider.Credential.Text (and similar fields) to one of three canonical labels. Matching is case-insensitive; MD is checked before DO so that "MD/PhD" maps to "MD" rather than falling through.

Usage

mysterycall_recode_credentials(x, other_label = "Other")

Arguments

x

Character vector of raw credential strings.

other_label

Character scalar returned when neither MD nor DO pattern matches. Default "Other".

Value

Character vector the same length as x. NA inputs return NA.

Details

Recognised patterns:

"MD"

M.D., MD, MD/PhD, MD-PhD, MD, PhD, doctor of medicine, allopathic

"DO"

D.O., DO, D.O.M., osteopathic

"Other"

everything else (PA, NP, RN, DDS, DMD, MBBS, …)

See also

Other provider characteristics: mysterycall_academic_patterns(), mysterycall_age_category(), mysterycall_assign_region(), mysterycall_classify_medical_school(), mysterycall_classify_practice_setting(), mysterycall_classify_ruca(), mysterycall_collapse_rare(), mysterycall_extract_physician_name(), mysterycall_government_patterns(), mysterycall_impute_age(), mysterycall_parse_certification_subspecialty(), mysterycall_reconcile_specialty(), mysterycall_reorder_by_freq()

Examples

creds <- c("M.D.", "DO", "MD/PhD", "D.O.", "PA-C", "MBBS", NA)
mysterycall_recode_credentials(creds)
#> [1] "MD"    "DO"    "MD"    "DO"    "Other" "Other" NA
